Blog

Information Measures

May 7, 2026 • Kaizhao Liu

This is a review of

Entropy

For a discrete random variable $X$ with probability mass function $P_X(x),x\in\cX$, the entropy of $X$ is

\[H(X)=\EE [\log\frac{1}{P_X(X)}]=\sum_{x\in\cX}P_X(x)\log\frac{1}{P_X(x)}.\]

Examples

Bernoulli

Divergence

Mutual Information

For a pair of random variables $X$ and $Y$, we define

\[I(X;Y)=D(P_{X,Y}\| P_XP_Y)\]

Mutual information measures the dependency between $X$ and $Y$

f-divergence

Tensorization

Inequalities between f-divergences
